title: Clarify installation readiness flow

sources.reason: Primary installation guide to revise for clearer readiness and verification steps.
Repository workflow guide for the active Work Item, checkpoint, and status commands.
Installed adoption checklist that the installation guide should reference more explicitly.
Canonical terminology for Work Item, adoption, and Cockpit Status wording.

riskAssessment.reason: Narrow documentation update with existing commands and checks already present in the repository.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for a doc-only change.

preReviewWarnings: Review command names and phase sequencing for accuracy against the Makefile and onboarding docs.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: docs/installation.md presents the installation flow as six phases: pre-flight, install, adoption, project calibration, validation, and first Work Item.
The guide explains the pre-flight checks, stack selection guidance, post-install file confirmation, adoption completion confirmation, calibration review points, and final readiness verification.
Every command named in the guide exists in the repository and the phrasing makes manual review points explicit rather than implying unattended acceptance.
guidelines: Keep the installation walkthrough accurate to the repository's published Make targets and scripts.
Do not widen the edit scope beyond the installation guide and its paired Work Item records.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
